outputs.desc: Output matrix [M, N] = A @ B + bias

strategies.notes: Split by output features; each thread computes subset of output columns.

constraints.notes: GEMM nt: C = A @ B^T. B is stored as [N, K] row-major. K and N should be multiples of 64.

kernels.notes: General matrix multiplication: C = A @ B^T + bias. This is the 'nt' variant; weights are stored as [N, K]. Used for projection layers in transformer blocks.
kernels.name: gemm_blocked_serial

constraints.notes: Q4_K uses 256-value super-blocks; K should be a multiple of 256.

strategies.name: token

strategies.name: feature
strategies.notes: Split by output features; good for decode.

constraints.notes: Q4_K requires K dimension multiple of 256 for dequantization. Most aggressive quantization for W2 (down) projection.

parity.notes: Compare against numpy matmul (higher tolerance due to quantization)
Compare against llama.cpp ggml_gemm_q4k

kernels.notes: General matrix multiplication with Q4_K weights and Q8_K activations. Used for W2 (down) projection in MLP. High compression ratio.
kernels.name: gemm_nt_q4_k_q8_k

constraints.notes: Q5_0 uses 32-value blocks; K should be a multiple of 32.

strategies.name: feature

constraints.notes: Q5_1 uses 32-value blocks; K should be a multiple of 32. B is transposed (nt = A * B^T).

constraints.notes: Q5_K uses 256-value super-blocks; K should be a multiple of 256.

kernels.notes: GEMM for Q5_K weights with FP32 activation input. No activation quantization needed. Q5_K: 5-bit super-block quant with nested scales/mins.

constraints.notes: Q6_K uses 256-value super-blocks; K should be a multiple of 256.

constraints.notes: Q8_0 uses 32-value blocks; K should be a multiple of 32.

constraints.notes: Q6_K uses 256-value super-blocks; K should be a multiple of 256.

parity.notes: Verify per-head RMSNorm matches PyTorch torch.nn.RMSNorm applied per-head

kernels.notes: Per-head RMSNorm on Q and K (Qwen3-style). Wraps rmsnorm_forward twice: once for Q with num_heads*num_tokens rows, once for K with num_kv_heads*num_tokens rows. Each row is [head_dim] elements. Gamma is shared across all heads (one gamma vector per Q, one per K). Placed between QKV projection and RoPE to stabilize attention scores.
kernels.name: qk_norm_forward

impl.c_declaration: void rope_precompute_cache(float *cos_cache, float *sin_cache, int max_seq_len, int head_dim, float base, int rotary_dim, const char *scaling_type, float scaling_factor);

parity.notes: Verify cos/sin cache matches PyTorch reference for multiple theta values (10K, 500K, 1M)

kernels.notes:
Precompute RoPE cos/sin cache for rotary position embeddings. Called ONCE at model init, not per-token. Different models use different base/theta values:
- Llama 2, Mistral: 10000.0
- Llama 3: 500000.0
- Qwen2: 1000000.0

RoPE Scaling:
- none: Standard RoPE
- linear: Scale positions by 1/factor (extends context)
- dynamic: NTK-aware dynamic scaling
- yarn: YaRN (Yet another RoPE extensioN)

Usage: codegen should call this in do_init() with the model's rope_theta from config, NOT hardcode the value.
kernels.name: rope_precompute_cache

params.name: base
params.desc: RoPE theta/base frequency (10000.0 for Llama2, 500000.0 for Llama3, 1000000.0 for Qwen2)

strategies.name: position
strategies.notes: Parallelize across positions; each thread computes a range of positions.

constraints.notes: head_dim should be even (uses head_dim/2 for rotation).

kernels.description: BPE tokenizer using trie data structure for UTF-8 text to token ID conversion

encode_params.name: text_len
encode_params.description: Length of input text in bytes (-1 for strlen)

vocab_offsets.description: Offset into vocab_strings for each token

steps: 1. Build trie from vocab_offsets + vocab_strings (or use prebuilt)
a. Walk trie to find longest matching token
b. If no match, apply BPE merges or use <unk>

implementation_notes: Trie can be prebuilt at model load time from vocab_offsets/vocab_strings
BPE merges needed for handling out-of-vocabulary subwords
Special tokens: <pad>, <unk>, <bos>, <eos> typically at fixed IDs
UTF-8 aware: must handle multi-byte characters correctly
kernels.name: tokenizer_bpe_trie
